betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Particle Chamber: Decay Chamber§f

§aDecay Chambers harvest secondary particles from unstable beam fragments!§f
The §bDecay Chamber§f is a square-hollow particle chamber multiblock that accepts an incoming particle beam, lets the particles decay or interact over the chamber's path length, and outputs §bnew particle species§f on its output ports. Use it to produce muons, pions, neutrinos, or other short-lived secondaries from a primary beam.

§6Multiblock Structure (DecayChamberLogic.java:58)§f:
- §bExterior§f: any size NxNxM (LengthX = LengthZ, square footprint), §cLengthX must be odd§f (3, 5, 7, …).
- §bCenter block§f: a §bParticle Chamber§f (target) tile at the geometric middle.
- §bBeam ports§f: one or more §bParticle Chamber Beam Ports§f on the outer faces, connected inward by a line of §bParticle Chamber Beam Blocks§f.
- §bMultiple input beam ports are allowed§f (the input is `>= 1` — see line 425+ for the input check).
- §bAt least 1 Particle Chamber Energy Port§f (supplies RF for the chamber to run).
- §bAt least 1 Particle Chamber Fluid Port§f for fluid in/out.

§6Controller Recipe§f:
The §bDecay Chamber Controller§f (`qmd:decay_chamber_controller`) is crafted in a vanilla §bminecraft.crafting§f grid from:
- §bElite Plating§f (`nuclearcraft:part:3`)
- §bTough Alloy Ingot§f (`nuclearcraft:alloy:1`)
- §bBasic Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:4`)
- §bDecay Hastener§f (`nuclearcraft:decay_hastener`) — provides the §bradioactive decay acceleration§f capability

§6Behavior in Operation (DecayChamberLogic.java:425-450)§f:
- The chamber processes the input beam in segments (one segment per port), computing the focus loss at each segment using `Equations.focusLoss(getBeamLength()/2, input)`.
- As particles traverse the chamber, unstable species may §bdecay§f into daughter particles (muons, pions, neutrinos, etc.).
- Output particles exit through OUTPUT beam ports with their new species identity and reduced focus.

§6Common Recipe Categories§f:
- §bPion production§f: high-energy proton beams on graphite/beryllium → pions → muons downstream
- §bBeta decay harvest§f: neutron-rich isotope beams → electron + antineutrino outputs
- §bAntimatter creation§f: high-energy proton-antiproton collisions in adjacent chambers feeding into a Decay Chamber

§cWarning§f: A Decay Chamber that is missing a target block, has no INPUT beam port, has no energy port, or is not square/odd-sized will fail the `isMachineWhole()` validation and refuse to form.

§eTip§f: Use the §bBeam Meter§f tool to read particle identity and focus at each port of a Decay Chamber in operation — this is the only way to verify the §bdaughter species§f produced by a decay chain without taking the chamber apart.
